channel.title: china-static:us
channel.description: Static RSS merge for china variant (us)

item.title: Mamdani's political gamble pays off as his endorsed candidates sweep their primaries
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.

item.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

item.title: In symbolic vote, Congress directs Trump to remove forces from Iran war
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.

item.title: Congress passes the largest housing affordability bill in decades
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: An expansive bipartisan bill intended to bring down the cost of housing by boosting the supply of homes has passed both houses of Congress, and is headed to the president's desk for a signature.

item.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

item.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.

item.title: A Revolutionary War soldier's DNA links him to living relatives
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: Continental Army soldier John Pumphrey enlisted as a teenager in 1777 and fought at significant battles before his death in action against the British in Camden, S.C.

item.title: Afghan Taliban hold first, closed-door talks with EU on deportations
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.

item.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

item.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."
